sources: Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics (AND) Position Paper: Vegetarian Diets (J Acad Nutr Diet, 2016) — doi:10.1016/j.jand.2016.09.025
NIH ODS Fact Sheets: Vitamin B12, Iron, Calcium, Zinc, Omega-3, Iodine, Vitamin D
ISSN Position Stand: Protein and Exercise (JISSN, 2017) — doi:10.1186/s12970-017-0177-8
note: Well-planned vegan and vegetarian diets are nutritionally adequate for all life stages (AND 2016). Attention to the nutrients below is sufficient to prevent deficiency.

vitamin_b12.risk_level: CRITICAL for vegans — virtually absent in plant foods
vitamin_b12.why_matters: Required for nerve function, red blood cell formation. Deficiency causes irreversible neurological damage.
reliable_plant_sources: Fortified nutritional yeast (check label — not all brands are fortified)
B12 supplement (cyanocobalamin 250 mcg/day or 2500 mcg/week are common doses)
vitamin_b12.vegetarian_note: Lacto-ovo vegetarians get B12 from eggs and dairy; still monitor levels annually.
vitamin_b12.source: NIH ODS — Vitamin B12 Fact Sheet

iron.risk_level: Moderate — non-heme iron from plants is less bioavailable than heme iron
iron.why_matters: Oxygen transport, energy metabolism. Low iron causes fatigue — common in active vegetarians.
iron.vegetarian_rda_multiplier: 1.8x the standard RDA recommended by the National Academies for vegetarians
iron.absorption_enhancers: Vitamin C (lemon juice, bell pepper, tomatoes) — increases non-heme absorption up to 6x
iron.absorption_inhibitors: Coffee, tea, calcium supplements — separate from iron-rich meals by 1–2 hours

calcium.risk_level: Moderate for vegans; lower risk for lacto-vegetarians
calcium.why_matters: Bone density, muscle contraction, nerve signalling.
calcium.note: Spinach and kale contain calcium but also oxalates that reduce absorption — don't rely on them as primary sources.

zinc.risk_level: Moderate — phytates in grains and legumes inhibit absorption
zinc.why_matters: Immune function, wound healing, testosterone, protein synthesis.
zinc.vegetarian_note: AND recommends vegetarians aim for 50% more than standard RDA
zinc.absorption_tip: Soaking, sprouting, or fermenting legumes reduces phytates and improves zinc absorption.
zinc.source: NIH ODS — Zinc Fact Sheet; AND 2016

omega_3.risk_level: Moderate for vegans — ALA from plants converts poorly to EPA/DHA
omega_3.why_matters: Heart health, inflammation regulation, brain function, recovery from training.
omega_3.conversion_note: Only ~5–10% of ALA (plant-derived) converts to EPA; <1% to DHA. Active individuals need EPA/DHA directly.
omega_3.epa_dha_vegan_source: Algal oil supplement — the only direct vegan EPA/DHA source (what fish eat to become rich in omega-3)
omega_3.recommended_dose: 250–500 mg EPA+DHA/day from algal oil (consistent with ACSM and AND recommendations)
omega_3.source: NIH ODS — Omega-3 Fact Sheet; AND 2016

vitamin_d.risk_level: Universal in low-sunlight regions; higher risk for vegans
vitamin_d.why_matters: Calcium absorption, bone health, immune function, muscle recovery.
vitamin_d.supplement_note: D3 is usually animal-derived; D2 and lichen-derived D3 are vegan alternatives.
vitamin_d.source: NIH ODS — Vitamin D Fact Sheet

iodine.risk_level: High for vegans who avoid dairy and iodised salt
vegan_sources: Seaweed (nori, kelp — variable, can be excessive)

complete_plant_proteins.notes: All 9 EAAs, highest DIAAS among plant proteins

complementary_combos: Rice + lentils / dal — classic Indian complete protein
protein_completeness.and_2016_note: Combining within a day (not necessarily in one meal) is sufficient to meet all amino acid needs.
protein_completeness.active_individuals_note: Athletes and regular exercisers should aim for upper range (1.6–2.0 g/kg) to compensate for lower plant protein digestibility (ISSN 2017).

quick_reference_checklist: B12: supplement daily (vegans) or monitor annually (vegetarians)
Iron: pair every iron-rich meal with vitamin C
Calcium: 3 servings fortified food or tofu per day
Zinc: soak legumes before cooking; eat pumpkin seeds regularly
Omega-3: algal oil 250–500 mg EPA+DHA if no fatty fish
Vitamin D: supplement Oct–Apr in low-sunlight climates
Iodine: use iodised salt or supplement if avoiding dairy
